summary.intent: Address Greptile round 3 code review findings on a deterministic compaction feature, fixing a P1 regression in the default compaction threshold and two P2 issues (dead code and algorithm semantics).
summary.outcome: All three findings resolved: default threshold restored to 0.95, dead code removed, greedy loop clarified with comments. All 245 chat tests pass and changes committed/pushed for round 4 review.

repo: Default compaction threshold was previously hardcoded at 95%; the new config schema must explicitly set 0.95 as the default to avoid silent behavioral regression on upgrade.

The greedy tail loop in shared.rs uses 'contiguous-tail' semantics intentionally — it does not cherry-pick scattered messages across gaps, which is a deliberate design choice distinct from 'maximum packing' semantics.

TEST_CONTEXT_WINDOW_TINY = 200 is used in recency_preserving.rs tests; changing the default threshold from 0.75 to 0.95 shifts the token budget (38 vs 30), affecting how many tail messages fit and breaking assertions.
StubProvider has its own context window constant in test_support.rs that must stay in sync with TEST_CONTEXT_WINDOW_TINY when threshold defaults change.

code.finding: default_compaction_threshold() must return 0.95, not 0.75, to preserve upgrade-neutral behavior matching the prior hardcoded trigger.
After the `if head_end >= tail_start { return }` guard, the `middle.is_empty()` check is unreachable dead code and was removed.
Greedy break in tail loop is correct for contiguous-tail semantics; clarified with a comment to prevent future 'fix' attempts.
TEST_CONTEXT_WINDOW_TINY value was adjusted so 0.95 default threshold still yields 2 tail messages, keeping test assertions valid.
StubProvider context window constant needed updating to stay consistent with the recency_preserving test window size change.

workflow: After changing a default config value, grep for all test fixtures and doc examples that assume the old default — they will all need updating.
Greptile automated review rounds (triggered via bash after each push) provide incremental findings; resolving threads and re-triggering is the established review loop.
A TLS blip caused a polling delay mid-session but resolved without intervention — keep polling rather than aborting.

friction: TLS blip interrupted the Greptile polling loop, requiring a retry.

Changing a single default value (0.75 → 0.95) cascaded into fixes across schema.rs, validate.rs, template.rs, docs, lib.rs, recency_preserving.rs, and test_support.rs — high blast radius for a one-line change.
Three tests failed after the threshold change due to implicit dependency on the old default math; required tracing through TEST_CONTEXT_WINDOW_TINY and StubProvider to find the root cause.
open_items: Round 4 Greptile review not yet completed — further findings may require additional fixes.

Test fixtures in recency_preserving.rs still use a magic constant (TEST_CONTEXT_WINDOW_TINY) tied to specific threshold math; consider making threshold explicit in each test config to decouple from future default changes.
